data.title: As a DSO agent session, I have CLAUDE.md kudos anti-gaming rules in place before any kudos reward trigger is active
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add kudos prohibition block and core overview section to CLAUDE.md before Story A (core infrastructure) ships. This is the P1 portion of the documentation split from Story H.
**Why**: The kudos system must have its own anti-gaming rules documented in CLAUDE.md before any trigger becomes active. Waiting until P4 Story H creates a window where the system operates without CLAUDE.md prohibition rules guiding agent behavior.
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md Kudos section with: system overview, prohibition block (listed before any reward descriptions), key constraint summary
- OUT: Detailed documentation (rank table with tier thresholds, jitter +/-20% disclosure, Goodhart Law disclosure) — those remain in Story H (P4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md contains a Kudos section with prohibition rules listed before any reward descriptions
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-9 (partial — prohibition block and overview)
- When this story is complete, the prohibition block explicitly names behaviors that disqualify an award (e.g., manual ledger writes, gaming commit messages, fabricating ticket creation dates)
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-9
- When this story is complete, the section ships concurrent with or before Story A completes — prohibition rules exist before any trigger is active
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding: ordering_violation — prohibition rules before first trigger ships

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
